title: Talks & Teaching
text:
Teaching
My teaching focuses on life course epidemiology, public health, social inequalities in health, and community health. I have contributed to teaching at the University of Fribourg, SSPH+, the Society for Longitudinal and Lifecourse Studies Summer School, and Université Paris-Est Créteil Val-de-Marne.

title: Publications
text:
Selected publications



Review · 2024


Life course epidemiology and public health

Wagner C, Carmeli C, Jackisch J, Kivimäki M, van der Linden BWA, Cullati S, Chiolero A.


The Lancet Public Health. 2024;9(4):e261–e269.

This Review examines how life course epidemiology changes the way chronic disease causes and prevention strategies are understood, with examples including hypertension, breast cancer, and dementia.

DOI PubMed Journal





Empirical article · 2024


Educational inequalities in multimorbidity at older ages

Wagner C, Jackisch J, Ortega N, Sieber S, van der Linden BWA, Chiolero A, Cullati S, Carmeli C.


European Journal of Public Health. 2024;34(4):704–711.

This study examines how parental and individual education shape inequalities in multimorbidity risk among older adults across 14 European countries.

Journal PubMed





Manuscript


Trajectories and inequalities in routine healthcare uptake from adolescence to retirement

Wagner C, Jolidon V, van der Linden BWA, Delaruelle K, Gauthier JA, Toma S, Bracke P, Burton-Jeangros C, Cullati S.


Manuscript in revision.

This study uses sequence analysis to examine long-term patterns of routine healthcare uptake across 14 European countries and how these patterns differ by life-course socioeconomic disadvantage.

title: Publications
text:
Additional publications



Empirical article · 2023


Intergenerational educational trajectories and inequalities in longevity

Wagner C, Cullati S, Sieber S, Huijts T, Chiolero A, Carmeli C.


SSM - Population Health. 2023;22:101367.

This study examines how parental and individual education shape inequalities in longevity among adults born before 1965 in 14 European countries.

DOI Journal PubMed

title: How does health inequality emerge across a life?
text:
Research strands


Life-course inequalities
How socioeconomic disadvantage accumulates across lives and generations, shaping health and healthcare trajectories.


Preventive healthcare systems
How screening, monitoring, and routine care are organized — and why unequal uptake can emerge even in universal systems.


Complexity and health equity
Using complexity-informed approaches to study feedback, timing, adaptation, and unintended consequences in health systems.

title: Research
text:
Cornelia Wagner

title: Research
text:
Research agenda


Life-course inequalities
I study how social and socioeconomic disadvantage shape health across lives and generations. This includes work on educational trajectories, family background, multimorbidity, longevity, and healthcare uptake.


Preventive healthcare systems
A central part of my current work focuses on preventive healthcare, including screening, routine monitoring, vaccination, and chronic disease management. I am interested in why prevention can remain unequal even in contexts with broad healthcare access.


Complexity and health equity
My emerging research agenda brings life course epidemiology into conversation with complexity science. This includes attention to feedback, timing, adaptation, linked lives, institutional contexts, and unintended consequences in health systems.

title: Research
Research
text:
Current directions
I am currently developing a research programme on unequal preventive healthcare trajectories. This work asks how social inequalities shape the timing, regularity, and continuity of preventive care, and how healthcare systems may unintentionally reinforce unequal patterns of use.
Future work will further connect life course research with complexity science, participatory systems mapping, and health insurance or registry-based data where possible.
See publications
